title: What is the difference between citizens and non-citizens: level 2
description: Test how well you apply your knowledge about the difference between citizens and non-citizens.
topic_path: What is the difference between citizens and non-citizens?
What is the difference between citizens and non-citizens: level 2

assessment_items.question_content:
Nneke’s parents were living in the United States studying to become doctors when she was born in Houston, TX. Both of her parents are Nigerian and are working towards getting American citizenship, but they haven’t gotten it yet. Nneke is turning fifteen soon and hopes that her parents become citizens soon. 

**Is Nneke an American citizen?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Yes; she was born in the United States, so she is automatically a citizen.
Yes; her parents are applying for citizenship, so she has temporary citizenship.
No; her parents were not American citizens when she was born, so Nneke is not a citizen.
No; Nneke has not applied for citizenship herself, so she cannot be a citizen.

assessment_items.question_content:
Chloe’s dad was born and grew up in St. Paul, Minnesota, before he joined the Air Force after high school. The Air Force sent him to Ramstein Air Base in Germany where he got married, retired from the military, and had Chloe. He has lived in Germany longer than he lived in the United States at this point. 

**Is Chloe an American citizen?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Yes; her father is an American citizen and lived in the United States for at least five years, Chloe is an American citizen.
Yes; she was born in a country allied with the United States, so Chloe is an American citizen.
No; her father has not lived in the United States for a long time, Chloe is not an American citizen.
No; she was not born in the United States and her father had retired from the military, Chloe is not an American citizen.

assessment_items.question_content:
Boris’s parents were on vacation in Puerto Rico when he was born. They were only in Puerto Rico for two weeks and returned home to Bulgaria a week after he was born. Both of his parents are Bulgarian citizens. Although they visit every couple of years, Boris and his parents have never lived in the United States for an extended period. 

**Is Boris an American citizen?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Yes; he was born in Puerto Rico, an American territory, Boris is an American citizen.
Yes; his family has made return trips to the United States, Boris is an American citizen.
No; his parents are not American citizens, so Boris is not an American citizen.
No; he and his parents have not lived in the United States for at least five years, so Boris is not an American citizen.

assessment_items.question_content:
Lucas was born in Sweden to two Swedish parents. When he turned eighteen, Lucas applied and attended college at a university in Boston, Massachusetts. He completed seven years of schooling in Boston after getting his law degree and is applying for jobs in the Boston area. 

**Is Lucas an American citizen?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: No; he was not born in the United States and neither of his parents are American citizens, Lucas is not an American citizen.
No; he has not given up his Swedish citizenship, so Lucas is not an American citizen.
Yes; he has lived in the United States for at least five years, so Lucas is an American citizen.
Yes; he received his education in the United States in a lucrative field, so Lucas is an American citizen.

assessment_items.question_content:
Alma and her family are from Tegucigalpa, Honduras. Life in Tegucigalpa has become dangerous, and it doesn’t look like things will get better. To protect her family, Alma applies for consideration as a refugee. She and her family get refugee status and move to Durham, North Carolina. 

**Is Alma an American citizen?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: No; despite getting refugee status, Alma still has to go through the naturalization process to become an American citizen.
No; Alma has to renounce her Honduran citizenship to become an American citizen.
Yes; Alma is a refugee, so she automatically became an American citizen when she stepped foot in the United States.
Yes; Alma has probationary American citizenship and once she has lived in the United States for at least five years, she’ll become a permanent citizen.

assessment_items.question_content:
Park Jung-kook was born in Washington, DC, to the South Korean ambassador to the United States. When his mother finished her term as ambassador after two years, the family returned to Seoul, so she could serve as the next Prime Minister of South Korea.

 **Is Jung-kook an American citizen?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: No; he was born to a foreign diplomat, making him ineligible for birthright citizenship.
No; he was born in an American territory, not a state, making him ineligible for American citizenship.
Yes; he was born on US soil, automatically making him an American citizen.
Yes; all foreign diplomats become American citizens when they serve and can pass their status to their children.

assessment_items.question_content:
Raqiya is fourteen years old and lives in Omaha, Nebraska. She and her family moved to the United States when she was four years old. Her parents started the process to become naturalized citizens as soon as possible, and they were just approved. 

**When Raqiya’s parents become American citizens, will she become one as well?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Yes; children under the age of eighteen inherit their parent’s status if they become naturalized citizens.
Yes; Raqiya has lived in the United States for at least five years, making her eligible for naturalization.
No; Raqiya was not born in the United States, meaning she cannot inherit her parents’ citizenship.
No; Raqiya needs to go through the naturalization process herself to become an American citizen.

assessment_items.question_content:
Vincenzo has played for Kettering Town FC, a professional soccer team, for the last five years. He’s just gotten an offer to play for the New England Revolution. To play for the team, he applies for a Green Card because he is moving to the United States due to his extraordinary soccer abilities. 

**Given the information above and your knowledge of government and politics, Vincenzo qualifies to become which category of non-citizen?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a lawful permanent resident
a refugee
an asylum-seeker
an undocumented immigrant

assessment_items.question_content:
Sakura’s family moved to the United States from Japan after her dad got an employment visa in 2005. When Sakura was ten, her dad lost his job and lost his visa, but they stayed in the United States. Now Sakura is eighteen and is applying for college. She’s never gotten arrested or charged with any serious crime, but she doesn’t know if she qualifies for the DACA program she’s heard about. 

**Given the information above and your knowledge of government and politics, could Sakura qualify for the Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) program?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Yes; Sakura is an undocumented immigrant brought to the country when she was young and has never been charged with a felony or misdemeanor.
Yes; Sakura came to the United States with authorization, and authorized immigrants may apply for the DACA program.
No; Sakura is eighteen, so she is too old to apply for admission to the DACA program, which is limited to children under sixteen.
No; Sakura entered the United States too late to qualify for the DACA program, as only people who entered before 2001 can join the program.

assessment_items.question_content:
Leon’s family entered the United States without proper authorization in 1999 when he was seventeen years old. He is the oldest of five children, so when he got to the United States, he didn’t go to school, choosing to work instead. He started his own family and eventually got his General Education Development credential (GED). He’s held a steady job building houses for the last twenty years, but he knows he can make more money if he gets proper work authorization. 

**Given the information above and your knowledge of government and politics, could Leon qualify for the Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) program?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: No; Leon was seventeen years old when he came to the United States, making him too old for the DACA program.
No; Leon went to work instead of going to school, which made him ineligible to become a DACA recipient.
Yes; Leon has been an upstanding member of society and has not gotten into any legal trouble, so he can apply for DACA.
Yes; Leon is an undocumented immigrant brought to the United States when he was a minor, making him eligible for the program.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ekaterina just turned eighteen and finished her schooling. She wants to find a job, but there are few available in her home country of Macedonia. She’s heard that she might have more luck in the United States. She makes it to the port of entry in Buffalo, New York and asks for asylum. 

**Given the information above and your knowledge of government and politics, does Ekaterina meet the requirements to seek asylum?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: No; Ekaterina did not fear for her life or fear persecution in her home country, and unemployment is not a way in which people can get asylum.
No; Ekaterina does not fill an employment need in the United States, so she is not qualified to seek asylum.
Yes; Ekaterina made it to a US port of entry before she asked for asylum, which means she can seek asylum.
Yes; Ekaterina’s quality of life in her home country would be very poor if she could not find a job which makes her qualified to seek asylum.

assessment_items.question_content:
Since 2016, Cameroon has been in the midst of a near-civil war. The Francophone central government has fought with the Anglophone minority and suppressed a series of peaceful protests. Nebane, a member of the English-speaking minority, fled Cameroon in 2017 after over twenty Anglophone people were killed. He made his way to the United States through South and Central America. When he got to the San Ysidro port of entry in San Diego, California, he asked for asylum. 

**Given the information above and your knowledge of government and politics, does Nebane meet the requirements to seek asylum?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Yes; Nebane left his country because he feared for his life, and he applied for asylum when he made it to the United States.
Yes; Nebane’s home country is involved in a civil war, which automatically qualifies him for asylum.
No; Nebane did not ask for asylum before entering the United States, making him an undocumented immigrant.
No; Nebane was not directly targeted by his government, so he is not eligible for asylum in the United States.

assessment_items.question_content:
In 2011, Syria plunged into a civil war. The Syrian government committed several crimes against their citizens, including unlawful arrests and killings. The Syrian Civil War forced Rania and her family to leave Syria and relocate to Turkey. Her family still struggled in Turkey, and they were interested in relocating to the United States so that Rania can pursue her dream of becoming a doctor. 

**Given the information above and your knowledge of government and politics, could Rania apply for refugee status?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Yes; Rania left her country because she and her family feared for their lives and persecution from their government.
Yes; Rania wants to be a doctor, and the United States needs more doctors, making her a highly qualified immigrant to the United States.
No; Rania is not in the United States and migrants cannot apply for refugee status until they reach the United States.
No; Rania’s race or religion were not the reasons her government might target her, making her ineligible for refugee status.

assessment_items.question_content:
After a hurricane hits the Dominican Republic, Edwin does not have a home and is struggling to find work anywhere on the island. He moves to Mexico to see if he can find work. Edwin wants to get help from the US government, but doesn’t know if he would qualify as a refugee.

**Given the information above and your knowledge of government and politics, could Edwin apply for refugee status?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: No; although Edwin is facing a challenging time after a natural disaster, he does not fear for his life and does not face persecution, so he’s not a refugee.
No; Edwin left his country before applying for refugee status, making him ineligible to become a refugee or receive any help from the United States.
Yes; Edwin cannot recover from a natural disaster in his home country, making him eligible to apply to the United States as a refugee.
Yes; Edwin had to leave his home country to Mexico to find new job opportunities, making him eligible to apply for refugee status.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In which of the following scenarios would someone have citizenship based on the principle of *jus soli*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Momoka was born in New York City, New York, while her parents were working there on a two-year contract as visiting professors.
Gideon was a lawful permanent resident in the United States, but he only lived there for six years before his family returned to Canada.
Roan’s mom passed on her American citizenship to Roan after he was born in France.
Hanelle entered the United States as a refugee when she was six years old, and she recently became a naturalized citizen.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In which of the following scenarios would someone have citizenship based on the principle of *jus sanguinis*?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Talib’s grandfather was an American citizen, but Talib was born and raised in Italy, just like both of his parents.
Luciano moved to the United States with his parents when he was eight months old and applied for naturalization when he turned eighteen.
Ana was born on Morón Air Base in Spain while her parents, who were lawful permanent residents, were serving in the United States Air Force.
Felicity was born in Seattle, Washington, but moved to Norway with her family when she turned thirteen.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following situations is an example of an action that citizens and lawful permanent residents can take that is well within their rights?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Antonia enlisted in the Navy as a sailor once she graduated from high school.
Cameron appeared in court for jury duty after she received a federal jury summons.
Jorge ran for Senator of his home state of Arizona after the incumbent retired.
Ahmet cast a vote in the 2020 presidential election, voting for the Green Party candidate.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following situations is an example of an action that citizens and lawful permanent residents can take that is well within their rights?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Malik applied for a bioengineering job at International Genetics, incorporated after graduating from college.
Oliver moved to Brisbane, Australia after he started graduate school at The University of Queensland.
Melisa became the Administrator of National Aeronautics and Space Administration after the president nominated her.
Isabella ran for an open position in her congressional district and became a member of the US House of Representatives.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following situations is an example of an action that citizens and lawful permanent residents can take that is well within their rights?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Aubrey buys an office building to start her law firm.
Deon applies for a job with the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA).
Maria becomes a federal judge after the Senate approves her nomination.
Paul joins the Republic of Korea Army to fulfill his service requirement.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following situations is an example of an action that citizens can take, but lawful permanent residents cannot?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Nolan votes in the 2022 midterm election hoping to get his political party more power in Congress.
Sekai buys her first house just a couple of miles from the hospital that she works at as a nurse.
Mekhi purchases a firearm after making sure they satisfy all the requirements in their home state of West Virginia.
Catalina organizes a protest at the city hall in Arlington to protest a recent law that requires new regulations on hotels and rentals.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following situations is an example of an action that citizens can take, but lawful permanent residents cannot?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Makena became a federal judge after the president nominated her for the position.
Zari applied for Medicare benefits after she turned sixty-five years old.
Khalil completed all the requirements and received his driver’s license.
Roger applied for a job as a paralegal for a major law firm in Atlanta.
